entities.name: HIV
entities.description: The human immunodeficiency virus that causes acquired immune deficiency syndrome

entities.name: AIDS
entities.description: Acquired immune deficiency syndrome caused by HIV

entities.name: SOUTHERN AFRICA
entities.description: A region in Africa with the largest HIV prevalence rate

entities.name: AFRICA
entities.description: The continent where more than 70% of the world's HIV/AIDS infected population lives

entities.name: LABOR MARKETS
entities.description: The systems of employment and labor supply impacted by the HIV/AIDS pandemic

entities.name: PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The efficiency of production, eroded by the loss of workers and cost of care for AIDS patients

entities.name: WELFARE
entities.description: The overall well-being and economic status of households and individuals, deteriorated by the pandemic

entities.name: LABOR FORCE
entities.description: The pool of available workers, reduced by HIV/AIDS and changing in age and sex distribution

entities.name: ECONOMY
entities.description: The economic system of a region, facing stagnation due to the pandemic

entities.name: ECONOMIC GROWTH
entities.description: The increase in the production of economic goods and services, reduced by the HIV pandemic

entities.name: INTERNATIONAL LABOR ORGANIZATION
entities.description: An organization that provides estimates on HIV infection among the prime labor force

entities.name: MEDICAL EXPENSES
entities.description: Costs incurred for medication and care of AIDS victims, reducing household welfare

entities.name: INCOME
entities.description: Money earned by household members, lost due to HIV/AIDS

entities.name: PROFITABILITY
entities.description: The degree to which sectors yield profit, eroded by the cost of caring for AIDS patients
